commands.repair_reason: A maintainer should close/ignore the withdrawn Dependabot version; automation should not rewrite a bot PR into a no-op dependency change.; Needs attention: The diff has a dependency provenance concern because it pins a direct production module to a withdrawn upstream version whose GitHub tag is no longer present.; Review finding: [P1] Keep crawlkit on the corrected v0.7.0 release — go.mod:39 (sha=ee729e0a903e5d05ceba365614e8b060a3bde818)
commands.review_summary:
This Dependabot PR updates the direct Go module dependency `github.com/openclaw/crawlkit` from v0.7.0 to v0.9.0 in `go.mod` and `go.sum`.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a dependency bump, not a runtime bug report. The source-level check is high-confidence because the upstream v0.9.0 tag lookup fails while current main and the crawlkit release notes point to v0.7.0.
commands.review_followup: A maintainer should close/ignore the withdrawn Dependabot version; automation should not rewrite a bot PR into a no-op dependency change.

You can also trigger a rebase manually by commenting `@dependabot rebase`. [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-start) [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-end) ---
Dependabot commands and options
You can trigger Dependabot actions by commenting on this PR: - `@dependabot rebase` will rebase this PR - `@dependabot recreate` will recreate this PR, overwriting any edits that have been made to it - `@dependabot show ignore conditions` will show all of the ignore conditions of the specified dependency - `@dependabot ignore this major version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this major version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this minor version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this minor version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this dependency` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this dependency (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself)
